project.name: MeshVase Slicer
project.description: Production-ready 3D mesh slicer that converts STL files into gcode with unique wave patterns for vase mode printing. Creates decorative mesh patterns by adding sinusoidal waves that alternate between layers to form gaps, designed specifically for lamp and vase printing business.

core_requirements.primary_function: Convert STL ASCII files into Klipper-flavored gcode using vase mode with meshified wave patterns
key_features: Sinusoidal wave pattern generation along perimeter (outward bulges only, never inward)
Layer alternation with configurable phase offset to create mesh gaps
Adaptive wave behavior based on geometry curvature and diameter changes
Base integrity preservation with three transition modes
Multi-threaded processing utilizing all M4 Pro cores
Interactive CLI with config file override support

wave_pattern_specifications.description: Core meshification logic - waves create outward bulges that alternate between layers to form diamond-shaped gaps
constraints: Waves MUST ONLY bulge outward from original perimeter
Inner diameter must be preserved exactly (critical for 90mm lamp bases)
Wave peaks extend outward by amplitude amount
Wave valleys stay at original perimeter (no inward deviation)

wave_amplitude.description: Distance from perimeter to wave peak (outward only)
wave_amplitude.note: Creates 4mm peak-to-peak when alternating layers offset by 50%

wave_spacing.description: Wavelength - peak to peak distance along circumference AND low to low on same layer

layer_alternation.description: Number of layers before wave pattern alternates phase
layer_alternation.note: 2 = alternates every 2 layers, creates tighter mesh. Higher = larger diamond gaps

phase_offset.description: Phase offset percentage between alternating layers
phase_offset.note: 50% creates perfect diamond gaps as shown in reference images

start_phase.note: random = organic look with no seam, aligned = vertical seam alignment

base_integrity_system.description: Ensures dimensional accuracy at base for mounting (typically first 28mm)

base_height.description: Height from bottom where special base handling applies

tighter_waves.description: More frequent oscillations but smaller amplitude in base region
tighter_waves.implementation: Increase wave frequency by 150%, reduce amplitude by 60%

fewer_gaps.description: Less aggressive mesh pattern - DEFAULT MODE
fewer_gaps.implementation: Gradually reduce wave amplitude from 0% at base to 100% at base_height

solid_then_mesh.description: Solid layers initially, then transition to mesh
solid_then_mesh.implementation: No waves for configured solid height, then transition

linear.description: Linear gradient from base to full mesh

exponential.description: Exponential curve - smooth visual transition - DEFAULT

curvature_adaptation.description: Waves adapt to model geometry - reduce waves on tight curves, full waves on straight sections
curvature_adaptation.detection: Calculate curvature by analyzing direction change rate per mm of travel

threshold_default.description: If direction changes > 30° per 10mm, trigger reduction

transition_smoothness.description: How quickly waves adapt when entering/exiting curves
transition_smoothness.implementation: Ramp amplitude over N segments where N depends on smoothness setting

diameter_scaling.description: Adjust wave count as layer diameter changes to maintain visual consistency

constant_wavelength.description: Maintain 4mm spacing - fewer waves on smaller diameters

dynamic.description: Adjust wave count using logarithmic scaling for smooth visual progression

vase_mode_mechanics.description: Maintain pure vase mode characteristics while adding wave patterns

gap_handling.travel_behavior: Maintain travel path even when not extruding

unsupported_geometry.detection: Automatically detect overhangs and unsupported sections
unsupported_geometry.behavior: Stop extruding in air but keep traveling in spiral path
unsupported_geometry.warning: Display warning about non-vase-suitable geometry, ask user to continue
unsupported_geometry.artistic_effect: Creates 'ghost' effect of original model where geometry doesn't support vase mode

notes.layer_height_ratio: 0.5mm layer height with 1mm nozzle allows steeper angles. Typical ratio is 0.5-0.8x nozzle diameter
notes.all_configurable: All settings must be easily editable in config.json with inline comments

extrusion_calculation.method: Calculate actual path length for each segment accounting for wave curves
extrusion_calculation.wall_thickness: Adjust extrusion rate to maintain consistent wall thickness
extrusion_calculation.extrusion_width: Use nozzle_diameter * 1.2 as baseline extrusion width

purge_line.description: 10mm straight purge line close to print (in addition to Klipper default)
purge_line.position: parallel to front edge of print
purge_line.purpose: Minimize leaking before actual print starts

centering.note: Ensure gcode centers model on build plate, not at origin corner

verbosity.requirement: Add detailed comments in gcode explaining each operation for easy troubleshooting
examples: ; Applying 60% amplitude reduction due to high curvature
; Base transition: 45% of full wave amplitude

manifold_check.on_fail: Display warning but allow continuation with user confirmation
manifold_check.message: Model is not manifold (has holes/gaps). Continue anyway? [y/n]

vase_suitability.action: Analyze if model is suitable for vase mode
vase_suitability.on_fail: Display warning: 'This model may not be suitable for vase mode. Continue? [y/n]'

dimension_check.action: Check if model exceeds build volume
dimension_check.on_exceed_z: Ask user: 'Model exceeds 280mm height. Squash Z-axis to fit? [y/n]'

squashing.method: Scale Z-axis only (X and Y remain 100%)
squashing.xy_check: After squashing, verify model still fits within 220x220mm footprint
squashing.warning: If XY exceeds after squash, display warning

alternative_modes: config file provides defaults for interactive prompts

workflow.step1: Display all STL files in current directory with numbered selection
workflow.step2: Interactive prompts for all parameters (reading defaults from config.json)
workflow.step3: Generate gcode with verbose progress output
workflow.step4: Display final command with all flags for easy copy/tweak
workflow.step5: Automatically launch OrcaSlicer with generated gcode

flag_support.description: All interactive parameters available as command line flags
flag_support.behavior: Flags override config.json defaults, skip interactive prompts for flagged parameters

final_output.description: After generation, display full command with all flags
final_output.purpose: User can copy and modify single parameter without re-running all prompts
final_output.example:
Generated! Command to reproduce:
python main.py --input lamp1.stl --nozzle 1.0 --layer-height 0.5 --wave-amplitude 2.0 ...

config_file_structure.format: JSON with inline comments for non-numeric string options
config_file_structure.requirement: All string options must have inline comments listing alternatives

contents: Model statistics (dimensions, layer count, estimated time)

preview_system.fallback: If OrcaSlicer not found, display path to gcode file for manual opening

multi_threading.detection: Automatically detect available cores on M4 Pro
multi_threading.strategy: Process geometry analysis and layer calculations in parallel
implementation: Divide layers into batches for parallel processing
Maintain sequential order for final gcode assembly

layer_processing.parallel_geometry: Calculate perimeters, curvature, and wave patterns in parallel
layer_processing.sequential_assembly: Assemble final gcode sequentially to maintain layer order

requirements: Graceful degradation for all error conditions
critical_errors: STL file not found or corrupted
Model exceeds build volume with no squash option

testing_requirements.validation: After each gcode generation, verify file contains actual gcode, not just headers
checks: Layer count matches expected from model height
No NaN or infinite values in coordinates

unit_tests.stl_parser: Test parsing various STL formats and error cases
unit_tests.geometry: Test curvature calculation, wave generation, diameter scaling
unit_tests.gcode_generator: Test output format, coordinate calculations, extrusion values

integration_tests.full_pipeline: Test complete STL to gcode conversion with various models
integration_tests.edge_cases: Test extreme parameters, tiny/huge models, complex geometries

approach.name: Phased_Implementation
approach.description: Build in phases with testing between each phase

phase: Phase 1: Project structure and configuration system
Phase 3: Geometry analysis (perimeter extraction, curvature calculation)
Phase 4: Wave pattern generation (core meshification logic)
Phase 5: Base integrity and transition system
Phase 6: Adaptive wave behavior (curvature and diameter adaptation)
Phase 7: Gcode generation and extrusion calculation
Phase 8: CLI interface and user interaction
Phase 10: Preview system and OrcaSlicer integration
Phase 11: Testing, validation, and error handling

requirements: Type hints for all function signatures
Comprehensive docstrings for all classes and functions
Meaningful variable names (no single letters except iterators)
Modular design with single responsibility principle
Robust error handling with try/except blocks
Logging at appropriate levels (INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
Constants in UPPER_CASE at module top

critical_implementation_notes.wave_direction: Waves MUST ONLY extend outward from perimeter. Calculate ideal perimeter point, then add wave offset in outward direction (perpendicular to perimeter, pointing away from center)
critical_implementation_notes.layer_alternation: Track current alternation phase. Every N layers (configurable), flip phase by offset percentage. Default: flip 50% every 2 layers
critical_implementation_notes.curvature_calculation: Use three consecutive points to calculate angle change. Store running average over small window for smooth transitions
critical_implementation_notes.extrusion_accuracy: Account for actual arc length in wave curves, not just straight-line distance between points
critical_implementation_notes.centering_math: Calculate model bounding box, offset all coordinates so center matches build plate center (110, 110)
critical_implementation_notes.vase_mode_spiral: Continuous Z increase while traversing perimeter - no discrete layer boundaries
critical_implementation_notes.performance: Use numpy for vector math where possible. Pre-calculate sine wave lookup tables for speed
